mnemonics: G.L.P. = Get Linear Pasts: A process where the present is a weighted sum of past shocks.
W.A.S.H. = Weights And Shocks History: The output is a Weighted Average of SHocks.
keyPoints: A general linear process is defined as X_t = μ + ∑(ψ_j * ε_{t-j}) for j from 0 to ∞, where ε_t is white noise.
It is a flexible model that represents a time series as a linear combination of past and present white noise shocks.
Many common time series models (like AR, MA, ARMA) are specific cases of the general linear process.
commonMistakes: Confusing the general linear process with a simple linear regression model. It is a model of the series itself in terms of its own innovations.
Assuming the process must be stationary. While often applied to stationary series, the definition itself does not impose stationarity; the weights {ψ_j} must be absolutely summable for stationarity.
Forgetting the constant term (μ) which represents the mean of the process when it is stationary.
